name: Compiled Obsidian Energy Metering Knowledge Base

entries.name: ABNT NBR 14519
entries.definition: Brazilian standard referenced for electronic meter interface characteristics such as optical communication.

entries.name: ABNT NBR 14522
entries.definition: Brazilian standard commonly referenced for electronic energy meter communication and data handling.

entries.name: Active Energy
entries.definition: Measured active energy, usually expressed in Wh or kWh.

entries.name: Activity Calendar
entries.definition: Calendar and special day configuration used for tariff periods and billing behavior.

entries.name: AES-GCM
entries.definition: Authenticated encryption algorithm referenced in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: Alarm
entries.definition: Meter indication for abnormal conditions that may need attention.

entries.name: Association
entries.definition: Logical connection between a DLMS/COSEM client and server with defined access rights and security level.

entries.name: Billing Profile
entries.definition: Profile storing metering quantities related to billing periods.

entries.name: Blue Book Part 1 OBIS
entries.definition: DLMS UA Blue Book Part 1 defines the OBIS object identification system used to identify COSEM object instances, measured data, abstract data, display codes, and medium-specific data items.
scope: relation between OBIS logical names and COSEM objects
entries.runtime_use: Use this entry to recognize requirements that cite the Blue Book OBIS object identification system or IEC 62056-6-1.

entries.name: Blue Book Part 2 COSEM Interface Classes
entries.definition: DLMS UA Blue Book Part 2 defines COSEM interface classes, including class identifiers, versions, attributes, methods, access semantics, and the relationship between interface classes and OBIS logical names.
entries.runtime_use: Use this entry to recognize requirements that cite the Blue Book COSEM interface class catalogue or IEC 62056-6-2.

entries.name: Bypass
entries.definition: Irregularity or tamper-related condition detected by the meter.

entries.name: Clock
entries.definition: Meter clock and calendar function used for time-based records and tariff periods.

entries.name: COSEM
entries.definition: Object model used with DLMS for representing metering data, configuration, and control functions.

entries.name: Current
entries.definition: Electrical current measurement.

entries.name: Disconnect Control
entries.definition: Meter function for remote or local disconnection and reconnection control.

entries.name: DLMS
entries.definition: Protocol suite used for utility meter data exchange and device management.

entries.name: ECDH
entries.definition: Key agreement method used in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: ECDSA
entries.definition: Digital signature algorithm used in DLMS/COSEM security suites.

entries.name: Error
entries.definition: Meter record for exceptional or failed operations.

entries.name: Event
entries.definition: Meter log entry representing occurrence of a defined operational, security, quality, or irregularity event.

entries.name: Firmware Update
entries.definition: Process or object set related to updating meter firmware and recording firmware changes.

entries.name: HDLC
entries.definition: Data link protocol used by some DLMS/COSEM communication profiles.

entries.name: High Level Security
entries.definition: DLMS/COSEM high-level authentication mechanism.

entries.name: IEC 62056
entries.definition: International standard family for electricity metering data exchange, including DLMS/COSEM application layer, OBIS, and interface classes.

entries.name: Load Profile
entries.definition: Time-series profile for measured or calculated quantities over configured intervals.

entries.name: Logical Device
entries.definition: Abstract entity inside a physical meter that exposes functionality through COSEM objects.

entries.name: LoRaWAN
entries.definition: Low-power wide-area network profile sometimes used for remote metering.

entries.name: Low Level Security
entries.definition: DLMS/COSEM low-level authentication mechanism, commonly password-based.

entries.name: LTE CAT-M
entries.definition: Cellular communication technologies and network interface cards used for remote metering.

entries.name: OBIS
entries.definition: Code system used to identify measured quantities and meter data objects.

entries.name: PLC-PRIME
entries.definition: Power-line communication profile for smart metering networks.

entries.name: QEE
entries.definition: Energy or power quality indicators and measurements.

entries.name: Reactive Energy
entries.definition: Measured reactive energy or related reactive quantities.

entries.name: Register
entries.definition: Meter data object used to store measured energy, demand, or related values.

entries.name: RTM 587/2012
entries.definition: Brazilian metrological regulation often referenced for electricity meters.

entries.name: SAP
entries.definition: Service access point identifier for DLMS/COSEM clients and logical devices.

entries.name: Smart Meter
entries.definition: Electricity meter with advanced measurement, communication, event logging, and remote management functions.

entries.name: SMF
entries.definition: Billing measurement system used in regulated electricity metering contexts.

entries.name: Snapshot
entries.definition: Recorded instantaneous or captured values from meter objects.

entries.name: Voltage
entries.definition: Electrical potential measurement, often phase or line related.

entries.name: Wi-SUN
entries.definition: Wireless communication profile used in smart utility networks.

entries.name: Association LN
entries.definition: COSEM object representing the current logical-name association, including object list, partners, context, authentication, and security setup references.

entries.name: Billing Profile
entries.definition: Profile and object group for storing energy and demand quantities used for billing periods.

entries.name: Blue Book Interface Class Catalogue
entries.definition: The Blue Book Part 2 interface class catalogue groups COSEM interface classes by purpose, including measurement data, access control, time and event control, payment, communication profile setup, Internet setup, PLC setup, ZigBee, Wi-SUN, and relation to OBIS objects.

entries.name: COSEM Access Rights Model
entries.definition: Model for defining whether each client role can read attributes, write attributes, or invoke methods on COSEM objects.

client_columns.meaning: service/local or specialized client depending on table context

entries.name: COSEM Logical Device Name
entries.definition: COSEM data object containing the unique logical device identification.

entries.name: COSEM Object Model
entries.definition: COSEM objects are instances of interface classes identified by logical names and defined by attributes, methods, access rights, and data types.

entries.name: Device ID 1
entries.definition: COSEM data object containing the electronic meter serial number.

entries.name: Disconnect Control Scheduler
entries.definition: COSEM schedule object used to trigger disconnect and reconnect scripts.

key_attributes.name: executed_script
key_attributes.meaning: script identifier for disconnect or reconnect

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Communication Profiles
entries.definition: Communication profiles considered for the DLMS/COSEM smart meter profile.

profiles.name: HDLC
profiles.medium: data link / serial or IP-related DLMS profile context

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Security Suites
entries.definition: Available DLMS/COSEM cryptographic suites and related algorithms.

entries.name: DLMS/COSEM Smart Meter Profile
entries.definition: Application and object profile for smart electricity meters using DLMS/COSEM services and COSEM objects.

entries.name: Event Group and Subgroup Model
entries.definition: Event model that classifies logs by group number, subgroup number, event number, and event description.

entries.name: High Level Security Association
entries.definition: Association security level using high-level authentication for management and measurement access.

entries.name: Image Transfer
entries.definition: COSEM object allowing firmware image transfer to COSEM servers.

entries.name: Load Profile
entries.definition: Time-series profile for load curve records and related measurement quantities.

entries.name: Local and Remote Association Matrix
entries.definition: Association matrix that maps client roles to management and metering logical devices and their required security level.

entries.name: Local Management and Measurement Client
entries.definition: Client role used for local access by a concentrator, collector, or central system. It generally has similar rights to the remote management client, with restrictions on remote association attributes.

entries.name: Low Level Security Association
entries.definition: Association security level typically using password-based authentication for read access.

entries.name: Management Logical Device
entries.definition: Mandatory logical device in a physical meter that exposes device management information, structure discovery, configuration, events, alarms, firmware update, and security-related objects.

entries.name: Metering Logical Device
entries.definition: Logical device that exposes the meter's measurement and billing functionality through COSEM objects.

entries.name: OBIS Logical Name
entries.definition: Logical name used to identify COSEM object instances, commonly represented as six OBIS value groups.

entries.name: OBIS Value Groups
entries.definition: OBIS logical names use six value groups in the A-B:C.D.E.F structure. The groups identify media, channels, data items, processing or classification, tariff or further processing, and historical or billing period allocation.

groups.meaning: Media or energy type; A=0 is abstract, A=1 AC electricity, A=2 DC electricity, A=4 heat cost allocator, A=5/6 thermal energy, A=7 gas, A=8 cold water, A=9 hot water.
Measurement or communication channel; B=0 means no channel specified, B=1..64 channel numbers.
Abstract or physical data item such as current, voltage, power, volume, temperature, list object, or data profile object.
Type, result of processing, or further classification for the item identified by A to C.
Tariff rate or other further classification, depending on the medium-specific object family.
Billing period, historical value, or other storage instance allocation.

standard_ranges.manufacturer_specific: Selected ranges such as 128..199 or 128..254 depending on value group and context.

entries.name: Power Quality Profile
entries.definition: Object and quantity group for power quality measurements and indicators.

entries.name: Public Client
entries.definition: Client role used to reveal the structure of an unknown metering device, typically through the management logical device without security.
limitations: No access to measurement data requiring authentication
No read or write access to password values

entries.name: Read Client
entries.definition: Client role used for reading parameters and energy data. Password access through LLS is typically required.
limitations: No read or write access to password values

entries.name: Remote Management and Measurement Client
entries.definition: Client role used by data concentrators, collectors, or central systems for remote point-to-point access to management and measurement functions.

entries.name: SAP Assignment
entries.definition: COSEM object listing logical devices and their SAP assignments.

entries.name: Security Policy Bit Map
entries.definition: Bit-level meaning of DLMS/COSEM security policy states.

bits.meaning: not used, must be set to 0
not used, must be set to 0

entries.name: Security Policy State Map
keywords: all messages are authenticated and encrypted
entries.definition: State-level map for DLMS/COSEM message authentication and encryption policy.

states.meaning: all messages are authenticated and encrypted

entries.name: xDLMS Services
entries.definition: Service set used by DLMS/COSEM clients to read, write, invoke methods, receive events, and receive pushed data.

services.name: ACCESS selective
services.purpose: Access selected data ranges or entries

entries.name: Data
entries.definition: Generic COSEM interface class for a single data value.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the object

attributes.name: value
attributes.meaning: Stored data value

access_semantics: The value data type is determined by the object instance logical_name and must be chosen so the value can be interpreted unambiguously.

source_refs.section: 4.3.1 Data (class_id = 1, version = 0)

entries.name: Register
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for a measured value with scaler and unit.

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Reset register value when supported

common_instances.name: Time threshold for long power failure

access_semantics: A Register models a process or status value together with its associated scaler and unit; the logical_name identifies the nature of the value.

source_refs.section: 4.3.2 Register (class_id = 3, version = 0)

entries.name: Extended Register
entries.definition: COSEM register with status and capture time in addition to value and scaler/unit.
behavior_notes: Extends Register with status and capture_time so the value can be qualified by status and capture timestamp.
reset sets value to the instance-specific default and sets capture_time to the reset execution time.

source_refs.section: 4.3.3 Extended register (class_id = 4, version = 0)

entries.name: Demand Register
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for demand values based on averaging periods.
behavior_notes: current_average_value is the running demand over the current averaging interval.
last_average_value is calculated over the previous number_of_periods multiplied by period interval.
period and number_of_periods define the demand calculation interval.

source_refs.section: 4.3.4 Demand register (class_id = 5, version = 0)

entries.name: Register Activation
entries.definition: COSEM class used to define active register masks and register assignments.

methods.name: add_register
methods.meaning: Append one Register, Extended register, or Demand register object to register_assignment.

methods.name: add_mask
methods.meaning: Add or overwrite one activation mask in mask_list.

methods.name: delete_mask
methods.meaning: Delete one activation mask identified by mask_name.

access_semantics: register_assignment and mask_list are static configuration data; active_mask is dynamic and selects the currently enabled subset.
add_register, add_mask, and delete_mask are action methods used to maintain tariff mask configuration without rewriting the whole object.
Changing active_mask changes which assigned registers participate in the currently active tariff structure.
behavior_notes: A Register activation object groups Register, Extended register, or Demand register instances for tariff handling.
Each mask in mask_list contains indices into register_assignment; the first assigned register is referenced by index 1.
active_mask names the mask that is currently active; registers assigned to the object but not included in the active mask are disabled.
Registers not included in any Register activation object's register_assignment remain enabled by default.

source_refs.section: 4.3.5 Register activation (class_id = 6, version = 0)

entries.name: Profile Generic
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for storing captured entries such as load profiles, billing profiles, event logs, and alarm/error logs.
behavior_notes: capture_objects defines the attributes copied into the buffer by automatic capture or the capture method.
Changing capture_objects clears the buffer so all entries remain homogeneous.
capture_period 0 means no automatic capture; values >= 1 define automatic capture period in seconds.

source_refs.section: 4.3.6 Profile generic (class_id = 7, version = 1)

entries.name: Clock
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for date, time, timezone, daylight saving, and clock status.

entries.name: Script Table
entries.definition: COSEM class containing executable scripts, each composed of action specifications.

entries.name: Schedule
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for schedule tables that define scripts to be executed according to configured times or calendar conditions.

methods.name: enable_disable
methods.meaning: Disable range A and enable range B.

methods.name: insert
methods.meaning: Insert or overwrite one schedule entry by index.

methods.name: delete
methods.meaning: Delete a range of schedule entries.

access_semantics: entries is static configuration containing script logical names, script selectors, switch time, validity window, weekday masks, special-day masks, and begin/end dates.
enable_disable, insert, and delete modify schedule execution state or table contents through action methods.
A Schedule entry executes at most one script; multiple scripts at the same instant are ordered by entry index.
behavior_notes: Schedule works with Special days table to model time- and date-driven device activities.
After power failure, missed entries are processed in order when they are still within their validity_window.
Forward time changes are handled like power failure recovery; backward time changes can repeat entries during the repeated interval.
Time synchronization shall avoid losing entries or executing entries twice; daylight-saving forward intervals execute missed scripts and backward intervals suppress re-execution.

source_refs.section: 4.5.3 Schedule (class_id = 10, version = 0)

entries.name: Special Days Table
entries.definition: COSEM class containing special day definitions for tariff/calendar behavior.

entries.name: Association SN
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for short-name association objects, access rights, and xDLMS context negotiation.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the Association SN object

attributes.name: object_list
attributes.meaning: List of objects visible in the short-name association, including class, version, logical name, and short-name base

attributes.name: associated_partners_id
attributes.meaning: Client and server SAP identifiers participating in the association

attributes.name: application_context_name
attributes.meaning: xDLMS application context negotiated for the association

attributes.name: xDLMS_context_info
attributes.meaning: Negotiated conformance, PDU size, and context parameters

attributes.name: authentication_mechanism_name
attributes.meaning: Authentication mechanism used by the association

attributes.name: secret
attributes.meaning: Association secret used by authentication mechanisms

attributes.name: association_status
attributes.meaning: Current association state

attributes.name: security_setup_reference
attributes.meaning: Logical name of the related Security setup object

methods.name: reply_to_HLS_authentication
methods.meaning: Completes high-level security authentication where applicable

methods.name: change_HLS_secret
methods.meaning: Changes the association secret used by HLS mechanisms

access_semantics: Association SN controls which short-name objects and access rights are visible to a client.
The object list and access rights define the implementable data model for this client association.
Secret and authentication fields are security-sensitive and should be handled as credentials.
Security setup reference links the association to security policy, suite, and key-management configuration.

source_refs.section: 4.4.12 Association SN (class_id = 12, version = 4)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with object list, association partners, xDLMS context, authentication, HLS methods, and security setup semantics.

entries.name: Association LN
entries.definition: COSEM interface class representing a logical-name association between client and server.
access_semantics: object_list exposes visible COSEM objects together with attribute and method access rights for the association.
security_setup_reference links the association to the Security setup object that manages its security context.
association_status indicates non-associated, association-pending, or associated.

source_refs.section: 4.4.4 Association LN (class_id = 15, version = 3)

entries.name: SAP Assignment
entries.definition: COSEM interface class listing SAP assignments for logical devices.

methods.name: connect_logical_device
methods.meaning: Connect or announce one logical device SAP assignment.

methods.name: disconnect_logical_device
methods.meaning: Disconnect or remove one logical device SAP assignment.

access_semantics: SAP_assignment_list maps logical device identifiers to logical device names.
The object is typically in the management logical device when a physical device hosts more than one logical device.
Association and wrapper-port binding logic can depend on SAP assignment when resolving logical devices.
behavior_notes: SAP Assignment provides the logical-device discovery list used before clients select an application association.
In TCP-UDP/IP profiles, application processes are bound through wrapper ports and logical device SAP assignments.
ABNT Appendix 9 exposes this instance at 0-0:41.0.0.255 with SAP_assignment_list readable by all configured client classes.

source_refs.section: 4.4.5 SAP assignment (class_id = 17, version = 0)
Table 8 general and service entry objects

entries.name: Image Transfer
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for transferring and activating firmware images.
behavior_notes: Image transfer is initiated, transferred block by block, verified, and then activated.
image_transferred_blocks_status tracks which blocks have already been transferred.
image_transfer_enabled controls whether the image transfer procedure is permitted.

source_refs.section: 4.4.6 Image transfer (class_id = 18, version = 0)

entries.name: IEC local port setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 19, version = 1) for configuring communication ports using the protocols specified in IEC 62056-21:2002. Allows modelling the configuration of the local (optical/electrical) port of a DLMS/COSEM device. Several ports can be configured (0...n instances).
access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name read-only for all.
default_mode selects the port protocol; when (1) or (2) is selected, most other attributes become not applicable.
pass_p1/pass_p2/pass_w5 are sensitive passwords per IEC 62056-21 and must be protected.
behavior_notes: IEC local port setup configures communication ports per IEC 62056-21:2002. Cardinality 0...n.
default_mode: enum (0) IEC 62056-21 modes A-E, (1) IEC 62056-46, (2) unspecified; (1)/(2) make most attributes N/A.
default_baud/prop_baud: enum 0-9 maps to 300...115200 baud.
response_time: enum (0) 20 ms, (1) 200 ms.
No specific methods; configuration via SET on static attributes.
Use this class when a requirement mentions local optical-port setup, IEC local port baud rates, IEC response time, or local-port passwords.
Treat password attributes as credential material even when source documents list them alongside ordinary communication parameters.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-25 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.7.1. Full attributes with access_rights, enum definitions, access_semantics, and behavior_notes.

entries.name: Activity Calendar
entries.definition: COSEM class for active and passive tariff calendars, seasons, weeks, and day profiles.

methods.name: activate_passive_calendar
methods.meaning: Copy all passive calendar attributes to their active counterparts.

access_semantics: Attributes ending in _active are the currently active calendar data.
Attributes ending in _passive are configured ahead of activation and become active through activate_passive_calendar or activate_passive_calendar_time.
Special Days table entries take precedence over Activity Calendar day-profile selection when available.
behavior_notes: Activity calendar models tariff structures using seasons, week profiles, day profiles, and script actions.
If Schedule and Activity calendar actions occur at the same activation time, Schedule-triggered actions are executed first.
After power failure only the last missed Activity calendar action is executed, and it must be ordered correctly with Schedule recovery actions.
day_profile_table entries reference Script table logical names and selectors for the actions to execute.

source_refs.section: 4.5.5 Activity calendar (class_id = 20, version = 0)

entries.name: Register monitor
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for monitoring or controlling device behavior through Register monitor functions.
access_semantics: thresholds, monitored_value, and actions are static configuration for threshold crossing behavior.
monitored_value references a simple typed attribute of Data, Register, Extended register, or Demand register.
actions has the same number of elements as thresholds; each action_set maps upward and downward threshold crossings to Script table actions.
behavior_notes: Register monitor executes scripts when the referenced value crosses configured thresholds.
The object requires a Script table instance in the same logical device.
Threshold values use the same type as the monitored attribute.

source_refs.section: 4.5.6 Register monitor (class_id = 21, version = 0)

entries.name: Single Action Schedule
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for scheduling one or more script executions at defined times.
access_semantics: executed_script references one Script table logical name and script selector.
type defines whether execution_time contains one or many entries, whether dates allow wildcards, and whether time values may differ.
execution_time stores time/date pairs; hundredths of seconds are zero for scheduled execution.
behavior_notes: Single action schedule models periodic action execution not necessarily linked to tariff calendars.
The object delegates actual behavior to the referenced Script table action.
Types 1 through 5 constrain the execution_time cardinality, wildcard use, and whether time values may vary.

source_refs.section: 4.5.7 Single action schedule (class_id = 22, version = 0)

entries.name: IEC HDLC Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 23, version = 1) for modelling and configuring communication channels according to DLMS UA 1000-2 Ed.11:2021 Clause 8 and IEC 62056-46:2007. Several communication channels can be configured (0...n instances).
access_semantics: All attributes are static channel configuration parameters, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client.
logical_name is read-only for all clients.
comm_speed enumerates 300 through 115200 baud values and may be overridden by entering HDLC mode through another protocol.
window sizes and maximum information field lengths may be negotiated to smaller values during logon.
behavior_notes: IEC HDLC setup configures one communication channel per IEC 62056-46. Cardinality 0...n.
comm_speed: enum 0-9 maps to 300...115200 baud; default 5 (9600).
window_size_transmit/receive: range 1-7, default 1, negotiable during logon.
max_info_field_length_transmit/receive: range 32-2030 bytes, default 128; max raised from 128 for efficiency.
inter_octet_time_out: range 20-6000 ms, default 25; max raised from 1000 for long-delay media.
inactivity_time_out: range 0-120 s; 0 disables disconnection processing.
device_address: one-byte addressing, 0x10-0x7D usable, 0x7E calling, 0x7F broadcast.

source_refs.section: 4.7.2 IEC HDLC setup (class_id = 23, version = 1)
5.7.2 IEC HDLC setup (class_id = 23, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-25 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.7.2. Full attributes with access_rights, value ranges, enum definitions, access_semantics, and behavior_notes.

entries.name: IEC twisted pair (1) setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring IEC twisted pair (1) setup parameters in DLMS/COSEM devices.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the IEC twisted-pair setup object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Operating mode of the IEC twisted-pair communication port

attributes.name: speed
attributes.meaning: Configured communication speed for the twisted-pair link

attributes.name: primary_address
attributes.meaning: Primary address used on the IEC twisted-pair bus

attributes.name: tabis
attributes.meaning: Timing parameter used by IEC twisted-pair communication

access_semantics: The setup object defines wired IEC twisted-pair communication parameters, including mode, speed, and bus addressing.
Address and speed writes affect physical-link interoperability and should be validated against the meter profile before applying.
Timing parameters should be treated as protocol configuration rather than metering data.

source_refs.section: 4.4.24 IEC twisted pair (1) setup (class_id = 24, version = 1)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with wired IEC mode, speed, address, and timing semantics.

entries.name: M-Bus slave port setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring the M-Bus slave port of a DLMS/COSEM device, including baud-rate capabilities and slave address state.

attributes.name: default_baud
attributes.meaning: Default communication speed used by the M-Bus slave port

attributes.name: available_baud
attributes.meaning: Baud rates supported by the physical M-Bus slave port

attributes.name: address_state
attributes.meaning: State of the M-Bus slave addressing procedure

attributes.name: bus_address
attributes.meaning: Configured M-Bus slave address

access_semantics: The setup object configures local M-Bus slave communication parameters rather than metrology values.
default_baud and bus_address are implementation configuration points and should be protected by the management access profile.
available_baud and address_state are read-only diagnostic or capability attributes.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention M-Bus slave port speed, supported baud rates, bus address assignment, or slave address state.
It complements M-Bus client/master setup classes; this object represents the local slave-side port setup.

source_refs.section: 4.7.6 M-Bus slave port setup (class_id = 25, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with M-Bus slave port baud-rate and address-state semantics for communication setup requirements.

entries.name: Utility tables
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for representing compact utility-defined tables whose cells are addressed through active and passive axes.

attributes.name: table_cell_values
attributes.meaning: Table payload values arranged according to the active and passive axis definitions

attributes.name: table_active_axis
attributes.meaning: Definition of the active axis used to address or interpret rows, columns, or intervals

attributes.name: table_passive_axis
attributes.meaning: Definition of the passive axis used together with the active axis to interpret table cells

access_semantics: table_cell_values is the main data payload and should be interpreted only with the matching active and passive axis definitions.
Axis attributes are configuration data; changing them changes the meaning of the stored table values and should be restricted to authorized clients.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention utility-defined tables, tabular measurement data, active axis, passive axis, or addressed table cells.
Requirements involving Utility tables should preserve both table value constraints and axis interpretation constraints; a cell value alone is usually insufficient for implementation.

source_refs.section: 4.3.7 Utility tables (class_id = 26, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with table payload, axis configuration, and implementation semantics.

entries.name: Modem configuration
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring modem communication profiles and initialization behavior.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the modem configuration object

attributes.name: comm_speed
attributes.meaning: Configured modem communication speed

attributes.name: initialization_string
attributes.meaning: Command string used to initialize the modem before communication

attributes.name: modem_profile
attributes.meaning: Profile of modem initialization and communication settings

access_semantics: The object stores modem initialization and speed parameters used before establishing a communication session.
Initialization strings are configuration commands and may contain vendor-specific modem control sequences.
Profile changes can alter remote communication availability and should be reviewed as communication configuration.

source_refs.section: 4.4.27 Modem configuration (class_id = 27, version = 1)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with modem speed, initialization string, and profile semantics.

entries.name: Auto answer
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for controlling how a device answers incoming communication calls.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the auto answer object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Auto-answer operating mode

attributes.name: listening_window
attributes.meaning: Time windows during which the device is allowed to answer incoming calls

attributes.name: status
attributes.meaning: Current auto-answer status

attributes.name: number_of_calls
attributes.meaning: Number of answered incoming calls or calls tracked by the auto-answer function

attributes.name: number_of_rings
attributes.meaning: Number of rings before the device answers

access_semantics: The auto answer object determines whether and when the meter accepts incoming communication sessions.
Listening-window configuration is time-dependent behavior and should be validated with clock/time-zone assumptions.
Ring and call counters support diagnostics but should not be confused with metering event logs.

source_refs.section: 4.4.28 Auto answer (class_id = 28, version = 2)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with incoming-call mode, listening-window, status, calls, and ring semantics.

entries.name: Auto connect
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for controlling automatic outgoing communication attempts.

attributes.name: logical_name
attributes.meaning: OBIS logical name of the auto connect object

attributes.name: mode
attributes.meaning: Auto-connect operating mode

attributes.name: repetitions
attributes.meaning: Number of repeated outgoing connection attempts

attributes.name: repetition_delay
attributes.meaning: Delay between repeated connection attempts

attributes.name: calling_window
attributes.meaning: Time windows during which automatic outgoing calls are permitted

attributes.name: destinations
attributes.meaning: Destination endpoints or identifiers used for outgoing connections

access_semantics: The auto connect object controls scheduled or triggered outgoing communication attempts.
Calling windows and repetition parameters define retry behavior and should be treated as operational communication policy.
Destination entries may include addresses or endpoint identifiers and should be validated before deployment.

source_refs.section: 4.4.29 Auto connect (class_id = 29, version = 2)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with outgoing-call mode, retry, calling-window, and destination semantics.

entries.name: COSEM data protection
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for binding COSEM data to protection metadata, protection parameters, and status used by secure data handling.

attributes.name: protection_buffer
attributes.meaning: Protected payload or protected representation of the associated COSEM data

attributes.name: protection_parameters
attributes.meaning: Parameters controlling how the protected data is generated, verified, or interpreted

attributes.name: protection_status
attributes.meaning: Current status of the protected data object and its protection process

methods.name: protect
methods.meaning: Trigger generation or update of protected data when supported by the implementation

methods.name: verify
methods.meaning: Verify protected data or protection metadata when supported by the implementation

access_semantics: protection_buffer is security-sensitive output and should be exposed only according to the configured association and security policy.
protection_parameters control security behavior and should require management-level write privileges.
Protection methods, when available, are active operations and should be treated as high-risk operations in requirements review.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention protected COSEM data, data protection status, cryptographic protection parameters, or protected payload verification.
This class belongs with security and access-control requirements, not ordinary measurement display requirements.

source_refs.section: 4.4.3 COSEM data protection (class_id = 30, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with protected payload, protection parameter, status, and high-risk operation semantics.

entries.name: Push Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring push operations, including the objects to push, destination and transport method, communication windows, retry behavior, and repetition delay.
behavior_notes: push_object_list defines the COSEM object attributes sent when push is invoked.
Push is sent using the DataNotification service when configured trigger conditions are met.
communication_window, randomisation_start_interval, number_of_retries, and repetition_delay define delayed execution and retry behavior.
push_protection_parameters define data protection options for pushed data.

source_refs.section: 4.4.8.2 Push setup (class_id = 40, version = 3)

entries.name: TCP-UDP Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 41, version = 0) for modelling the setup of the TCP or UDP sub-layer of the COSEM TCP/UDP based transport layer (TCP-UDP/IP profile) or the UDP sub-layer of the DLMS/COSEM CoAP transport layer. Cardinality 0...n.
access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name is read-only.
TCP-UDP_port defines the TCP/UDP or CoAP UDP endpoint port (IANA 4059) used by the physical device.
IP_reference points to the IPv4 or IPv6 setup object that owns IP-layer address settings.
MSS is advertised only in the initial TCP SYN and is not negotiated by this attribute.
behavior_notes: TCP-UDP setup models the TCP or UDP sub-layer for TCP-UDP/IP and CoAP profiles. Cardinality 0...n; one instance per data link layer.
Within a physical device, each client AP or server logical device is bound to a wrapper port with help from SAP Assignment.
nb_of_sim_conn limits simultaneous TCP/UDP connections supported by the transport layer.
inactivity_time_out aborts inactive TCP connections; zero disables this timeout.

source_refs.section: 4.9.1 TCP-UDP setup (class_id = 41, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-26 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.9.1. Full attributes with access_rights, value ranges, access_semantics, and behavior_notes.

entries.name: IPv4 Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 42, version = 0) for modelling the setup of the IPv4 layer, handling all information related to the IP address settings of a given device and lower layer connection. There shall be one instance per network interface implemented (cardinality 0...n).

methods.name: add_mc_IP_address
methods.meaning: Add one multicast IPv4 address.

methods.name: delete_mc_IP_address
methods.meaning: Delete one multicast IPv4 address.

methods.name: get_nbof_mc_IP_addresses
methods.meaning: Return the number of configured multicast IPv4 addresses.

access_semantics: logical_name and static config attributes (DL_reference, use_DHCP_flag) are RW via SET by management client; logical_name read-only for all.
Dynamic address attributes (IP_address, multicast, subnet_mask, gateway, DNS) are RW but runtime values may reflect DHCP negotiation when use_DHCP_flag is true.
DL_reference points to the lower data-link setup object used by this IPv4 interface.
multicast_IP_address holds class-D multicast addresses (224.0.0.0 - 239.255.255.255) accepted by the device.
behavior_notes: A device has one IPv4 setup instance per network interface using TCP-UDP/IPv4. Cardinality 0...n.
IPv4 addresses are encoded as double-long-unsigned, e.g. 192.168.0.1 = C0A80001 = 3232235521.
IP_address may be static or dynamic (DHCP); 0 means no address assigned.
IP_options may carry RFC 791 options (security, source route, record route, internet timestamp).

source_refs.section: 4.9.2 IPv4 setup (class_id = 42, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-26 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.9.2. Full attributes with access_rights, method behavior, access_semantics, and behavior_notes.

entries.name: MAC address setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for exposing or configuring the network MAC address used by DLMS/COSEM communication profiles.

attributes.name: MAC_address
attributes.meaning: Layer-2 MAC address assigned to the communication interface

access_semantics: MAC_address identifies the local layer-2 network interface and is usually read by clients for communication inventory.
Write access to MAC_address, when supported by a profile, should be limited to management clients because it changes network identity.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention MAC address setup, Ethernet physical address, or layer-2 interface identity.
It is normally used together with TCP-UDP, IPv4, IPv6, PPP, or other communication setup classes.

source_refs.section: 4.7.21 MAC address setup (class_id = 43, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Expanded with MAC_address attribute and layer-2 identity semantics.

entries.name: PPP setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 44, version = 0) for modelling the setup of interfaces using the PPP protocol, handling all information related to PPP settings of a given physical device and lower layer connection. One instance per network interface (cardinality 0...n).
access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name read-only for all.
PHY_reference points to the physical layer interface object supporting the PPP layer.
LCP_options and IPCP_options govern PPP link and IP-layer negotiation per IETF STD 51 / RFC 1661.
PPP_authentication carries authentication parameters (e.g. PAP/CHAP).
behavior_notes: A device has one PPP setup instance per network interface. Cardinality 0...n.
LCP_options: array of {Type, Length, Data} structures; MRU default 1500 octets, Authentication-Protocol default no auth.
IPCP_options: array of IP-layer negotiation option structures.
PPP_authentication: authentication type/parameters per PPP_auth_type.

source_refs.section: 4.9.5 PPP setup (class_id = 44, version = 0)

entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-26 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.9.5. Full attributes with access_rights, LCP option enum definitions, access_semantics, and behavior_notes.

entries.name: GPRS modem setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 45, version = 0) for setting up GPRS modems, handling all data necessary for modem management. Cardinality 0...n.
access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name read-only for all.
APN identifies the GPRS network access point.
PIN_code is the SIM PIN (sensitive, required for SIM authentication).
quality_of_service carries GPRS QoS parameters affecting modem connectivity.
behavior_notes: GPRS modem setup handles all data necessary for GPRS modem management. Cardinality 0...n.
APN: Access Point Name for the GPRS network.
PIN_code: SIM card PIN (long-unsigned); sensitive.
quality_of_service: structure of GPRS QoS parameters (peak/mean throughput, reliability class).

source_refs.section: 4.7.7 GPRS modem setup (class_id = 45, version = 0)

entries.name: SMTP setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 46, version = 0) for setting up data exchange using the SMTP protocol. Cardinality 0...n.
access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name read-only for all.
server_port defaults to IANA SMTP port 25.
login_password is sensitive; void string means no authentication.
server_address/sender_address may be DNS names or dotted-IP octet-strings.
behavior_notes: SMTP setup allows data exchange using the SMTP protocol. Cardinality 0...n.
server_port: default 25 (IANA smtp 25/tcp, 25/udp).
login_password: void string = no authentication; otherwise sensitive.
server_address/sender_address: DNS-resolvable name or dotted IP format.

source_refs.section: 4.9.6 SMTP setup (class_id = 46, version = 0)

entries.name: GSM diagnostic
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 47, version = 2). Stores GSM/GPRS, UMTS, CDMA or LTE cellular network parameters (registration status, signal quality, cell info) for diagnosing communication problems.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Stores GSM/GPRS, UMTS, CDMA or LTE cellular network parameters (registration status, signal quality, cell info) for diagnosing communication problems.
No specific methods defined.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.7.8; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: IPv6 Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 48, version = 0) for modelling the setup of the IPv6 layer, handling all information related to the IPv6 address settings of a given device and lower layer connection. One instance per network interface (cardinality 0...n).

methods.name: add_IPv6_address
methods.meaning: Add one IPv6 address.

methods.name: remove_IPv6_address
methods.meaning: Remove one IPv6 address.

access_semantics: logical_name is read-only for all clients; static config attributes are RW via SET by management client.
DL_reference links the IPv6 setup object to the lower-layer communication setup object.
address_config_mode defines the IPv6 address configuration mechanism (auto/DHCPv6/manual/ND); common for all addresses of this instance.
unicast_IPv6_addresses is managed via add/remove methods; empty array = no address or reset.
behavior_notes: A device has one IPv6 setup instance per network interface. Cardinality 0...n.
address_config_mode: enum 0=auto-config (default), 1=DHCPv6, 2=Manual, 3=ND.
unicast/multicast/gateway IPv6 addresses follow RFC 3513 format; empty array resets all.
traffic_class: range 0-63, default 0.
neighbor_discovery_setup holds ND parameters.

source_refs.section: 4.9.3 IPv6 setup (class_id = 48, version = 0)

entries.name: S-FSK Phy&MAC set-up
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 50, version = 1). Stores the data necessary to set up and manage the physical and the MAC layer of the PLC S-FSK lower layer profile. (The use of version 0 of this interface class is deprecated.)
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: An instance stores the data necessary to set up and manage the physical and the MAC layer of the PLC S-FSK lower layer profile (physical-layer management and MAC-layer management variables).
Specific methods: none defined.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.3; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: S-FSK Active initiator
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 51, version = 0). Stores the data of the active initiator, i.e. the client system which has last registered the server system with a CIASE Register request.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Stores the identifiers of the active initiator (system title, MAC address and L-SAP selector), i.e. the client system that has last registered the server.
The value of active_initiator can be updated by the reset_NEW_not_synchronized method or by the CIASE Register service.
Specific methods: reset_NEW_not_synchronized.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.4; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: S-FSK MAC synchronization timeouts
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 52, version = 0). Stores the timeouts related to the S-FSK MAC synchronization process.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds the four timeouts of the synchronization process: search-initiator, synchronization-confirmation, time-out-not-addressed and time-out-frame-not-OK. A value of 0 cancels the use of the related counter.
Specific methods: none defined.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.5; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: S-FSK MAC counters
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 53, version = 0). Stores counters related to the S-FSK frame exchange, transmission and repetition phases.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds synchronization and desynchronization statistics plus per-cause counters for broadcast frames, repetitions, transmissions and CRC-OK/CRC-NOK frames; all counters wrap to 0 at the maximum value.
The reset method clears all counters.
Specific methods: reset.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.6; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: IEC 61334-4-32 LLC setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 55, version = 1). Holds parameters necessary to set up and manage the LLC layer as specified in IEC 61334-4-32:1996.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds the LLC frame length and the reply status list (L-SAPs with a not-empty, not-yet-read RDR buffer), the latter being locally generated by the LLC sub layer.
Specific methods: none defined.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.7; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: S-FSK Reporting system list
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 56, version = 0). Holds the list of reporting systems (server systems that have made a DiscoverReport request and are not yet registered).
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds the system-titles of server systems that have made a DiscoverReport request and are not yet registered; the list is finite, newest-first, and a system-title appears at most once.
The list is updated on DiscoverReport (add) and Register (delete) CI_PDU reception.
Specific methods: none defined.
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.10.8; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: ISO/IEC 8802-2 LLC Type 1 setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 57, version = 0). Holds the parameters necessary to set up the ISO/IEC 8802-2 LLC layer in Type 1 operation.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds the maximum number of octets in a UI PDU; all MACs shall at least accommodate UI PDU information fields of 128 octets.
Specific methods: none defined.

entries.name: ISO/IEC 8802-2 LLC Type 2 setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 58, version = 0). Holds the parameters necessary to set up the ISO/IEC 8802-2 LLC layer in Type 2 operation.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.

entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 59, version = 0). Holds the parameters necessary to set up the ISO/IEC 8802-2 LLC layer in Type 3 operation.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: Register Table
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for representing register values in a table-oriented structure, typically used where related measured values are arranged as rows and columns.

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Clear table_cell_values without changing captured attributes

methods.name: capture
methods.meaning: Copy configured attribute values into table_cell_values

behavior_notes: table_cell_definition defines the common class_id, logical_name wildcard, group_E_values, and attribute_index for captured attributes.
Changing table_cell_definition automatically resets table_cell_values and propagates to profiles that capture this object.
When capturing Register or Extended register value attributes, scaler_unit is common for all objects and holds a copy.

entries.name: Compact Data
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for compact storage and transfer of captured values using a template description and compact buffer representation.

template_id and template_description let a client reconstruct uncompacted attribute descriptors, data types, and values.
The template_id attribute shall be the first element in capture_objects.
Relative and absolute selective access mechanisms are mutually exclusive for captured Profile generic buffers.

entries.name: Status mapping
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 63, version = 0) for modelling the mapping of bits in a status word to entries in a reference table. Cardinality 0...n.
access_semantics: logical_name and mapping_table are static RW via SET by management client; logical_name read-only for all.
status_word is dynamic, read-only (R); reflects current runtime value of the monitored status word.
mapping_table.ref_table_id identifies the reference status table for bit-to-entry mapping.
behavior_notes: Status mapping models the mapping of bits in a status word to entries in a reference table. Cardinality 0...n.
status_word: dynamic, CHOICE of bit-string/integer/octet-string etc.; size n*8 bits, max 65536 bits; always interpreted as bit-string.
mapping_table: {ref_table_id, ref_table_mapping CHOICE}. long-unsigned = sequential bit-to-entry; array = explicit mapping.

entries.name: Security Setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for security policy, security suite, system titles, certificates, and key management methods.

common_instances.name: Security Setup for remote client association
Security Setup for local client association

access_semantics: Instances contain the security suite and policy applicable between a client and server identified by their system titles.
The class provides methods to increase security level and manage symmetric keys, asymmetric key pairs, and certificates.

entries.name: Parameter monitor
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 65, version = 1) for monitoring configuration changes. Holds parameter_list_name, hash_algorithm_id, parameter_value_digest, and parameter values so clients can detect configuration drift via digest comparison. Cardinality 0...n.

methods.name: add_parameter
methods.meaning: Add one parameter reference to parameter_list.

methods.name: delete_parameter
methods.meaning: Delete one parameter reference from parameter_list.

access_semantics: logical_name and static config attributes (parameter_list, parameter_list_name, hash_algorithm_id) are RW via SET by management client; logical_name read-only for all.
Dynamic attributes (changed_parameter, capture_time, parameter_value_digest, parameter_values) are read-only (R) — reflect runtime state.
parameter_list defines the monitored class_id, logical_name, and attribute_index references; managed via add/delete methods.
parameter_value_digest lets clients compare a known configuration snapshot before reading parameter_values.
behavior_notes: Parameter monitor supports configuration-change detection for a defined list of COSEM attributes. Cardinality 0...n.
changed_parameter/capture_time report the most recent change (dynamic, read-only).
The digest is calculated over parameter values in parameter_list order using hash_algorithm_id.
parameter_values holds A-XDR encoded copies of referenced attributes and may be captured into Profile generic buffers.

source_refs.section: 4.5.10 Parameter monitor (class_id = 65, version = 1)

entries.name: Measurement data monitoring objects
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 66, version = 0) for capturing waveform data of a monitored quantity (voltage or current) around a trigger event. One instance holds samples of only one measured quantity at a time. Cardinality 0...n.

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Reset the waveform capture.

methods.name: trigger
methods.meaning: Initiate a waveform capture (manual or register-monitor driven).

access_semantics: logical_name and static config (sampling_rate, before/after sample counts, scaler_unit) are RW via SET by management client; logical_name read-only for all.
Dynamic attributes (waveform_data, trigger_time, trigger_source, status) are read-only (R) — reflect runtime capture state.
Capture only enabled when number_of_samples_before/after_trigger are non-zero.
behavior_notes: Captures waveform samples of one measured quantity around a trigger event. One quantity per instance. Cardinality 0...n.
waveform_data: dynamic compact-array; total samples = before + after window.
trigger_time: microseconds clock precision for exact capture timing.
trigger_source: {origin remote/local-script, cause}; trigger method manual or register-monitor driven.
waveform_data is a Profile generic capture candidate.

source_refs.section: 4.3.11 Measurement data monitoring objects (class_id = 66, version = 0)

entries.name: Sensor manager
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 67, version = 0) for managing detailed information related to a sensor (mainly non-electricity metering). Combines nameplate/site data, an Extended-register function for raw values, and Register-monitor functions for raw and processed values. Cardinality 0...n.

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Reset the sensor manager.

access_semantics: logical_name read-only for all; all other attributes dynamic.
Nameplate/site data (attr 2-6) and runtime values (raw_value, status, capture_time, processed_value) are read-only (R).
Monitor thresholds/actions (attr 11-12, 14-15) are RW via SET by management client where configurable.
Not all modules necessarily present; unused attributes may be unimplemented.
behavior_notes: Sensor manager manages complex sensor information, mainly for non-electricity metering (MID). No OBIS codes. Cardinality 0...n.
Nameplate/site data: serial_number, metrological_identification, output_type, adjustment_method, sealing_method.
Extended register for raw value (attr 7-10): raw data without own OBIS code included here.
Register monitor for raw value (attr 11-12) and processed value (attr 13-15).
Not all modules necessarily present.

entries.name: Arbitrator
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 68, version = 0). Determines, from pre-configured permissions and weightings, which action is carried out when multiple actors may request potentially conflicting actions to control the same resource.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Determines, from pre-configured permissions_table and weightings_table, which action wins when multiple actors request conflicting actions on a single resource (one Arbitrator per controlled resource).

Actions are requested via request_action; the AP checks the actor's permissions, updates most_recent_requests_table, sums the weightings, and on a unique highest total weight writes last_outcome and executes the corresponding Script table script.
Specific methods: request_action (mandatory), reset (optional).
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.5.12; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

entries.name: Disconnect Control
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for disconnect/reconnect control.

state_model.control_mode_note: control_mode configures which remote, manual, and local transitions are allowed.

behavior_notes: remote_disconnect forces the object into Disconnected when remote disconnection is enabled.
remote_reconnect either reconnects directly or moves to Ready_for_reconnection depending on control_mode.
The object has no command memory; commands are executed immediately.

source_refs.section: 4.5.8 Disconnect control (class_id = 70, version = 1)

entries.name: Limiter
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for monitoring a value against thresholds and invoking configured actions when over-threshold, under-threshold, or emergency profile conditions apply.
behavior_notes: monitored_value identifies the object attribute monitored by the limiter and only simple data types are allowed.
threshold_active follows threshold_normal or threshold_emergency depending on whether an emergency profile is active.
over-threshold and under-threshold actions are executed only after the configured minimum duration is exceeded.

source_refs.section: 4.5.9 Limiter (class_id = 71, version = 0)

entries.name: M-Bus client
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for representing and managing data collected from an M-Bus device through a DLMS/COSEM server.

attributes.name: m_bus_port_reference
attributes.meaning: Reference to the M-Bus port setup object used for this client

attributes.name: capture_period
attributes.meaning: Periodic capture interval for values read from the attached M-Bus device

attributes.name: primary_address
attributes.meaning: Primary M-Bus address of the attached slave device

attributes.name: identification_number
attributes.meaning: Identification number reported by the M-Bus device

attributes.name: manufacturer_id
attributes.meaning: Manufacturer identifier of the attached M-Bus device

attributes.name: data_header
attributes.meaning: Header metadata for the captured M-Bus data set

attributes.name: value_information
attributes.meaning: Value information blocks describing captured M-Bus values

methods.name: slave_install
methods.meaning: Install or bind an M-Bus slave device to the client object

methods.name: slave_deinstall
methods.meaning: Remove the binding between the client object and an M-Bus slave device

methods.name: capture
methods.meaning: Trigger capture of data from the attached M-Bus device

access_semantics: m_bus_port_reference and primary_address determine the physical slave selected by the client object and require controlled write access.
capture_period controls polling frequency and can affect bus load and battery-powered slave behavior.
Install, deinstall, and capture methods are active operations and should be reviewed as operational commands.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention M-Bus client, attached M-Bus slave, primary address, capture period, manufacturer ID, or imported submeter data.
M-Bus client requirements often combine communication setup constraints with measurement capture and data mapping constraints.

entries.name: M-Bus master port setup
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for configuring M-Bus master port setup parameters in DLMS/COSEM devices.

attributes.name: comm_speed
attributes.meaning: M-Bus communication speed used by the master port

attributes.name: m_bus_profile
attributes.meaning: M-Bus profile or mode used on the port when the implementation supports multiple profiles

attributes.name: active_devices
attributes.meaning: List or count of active M-Bus devices known on the master port

access_semantics: comm_speed must match the attached M-Bus segment and should be writable only through authorized communication configuration.
active_devices is diagnostic/inventory information and should be interpreted together with M-Bus client objects.
Changing port setup can temporarily disrupt reads from all clients using the same M-Bus master port.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention M-Bus master port speed, bus profile, active devices, or wired M-Bus segment setup.

entries.name: DLMS server M-Bus port setup

attributes.name: comm_speed
attributes.meaning: Communication speed used by the DLMS server M-Bus port

attributes.name: server_primary_address
attributes.meaning: Primary address used when the DLMS server exposes itself through an M-Bus port

attributes.name: identification_number
attributes.meaning: Identification number exposed through the server-side M-Bus port

access_semantics: comm_speed and server_primary_address affect external reachability through the M-Bus server port and require controlled write access.
Identification values should be aligned with device identity and installation requirements.
Server-side M-Bus port setup is distinct from M-Bus client configuration used to read attached slave devices.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention DLMS server access over M-Bus, server M-Bus primary address, or M-Bus port identity.
Pair these requirements with association and communication profile requirements when external clients access the server through M-Bus.

entries.name: M-Bus diagnostic
entries.definition: COSEM interface class for exposing M-Bus diagnostic status, error counters, and communication health information.

attributes.name: received_frames_counter
attributes.meaning: Counter for M-Bus frames received by the diagnostic object

attributes.name: transmitted_frames_counter
attributes.meaning: Counter for M-Bus frames transmitted by the diagnostic object

attributes.name: error_counter
attributes.meaning: Counter for detected M-Bus communication errors

attributes.name: last_communication_status
attributes.meaning: Last known M-Bus communication status or failure reason

methods.name: reset
methods.meaning: Reset diagnostic counters when supported by the implementation

access_semantics: Diagnostic counters are read-only operational evidence used for maintenance, installation, and troubleshooting.
Counter reset is an active maintenance operation and should be restricted or audited.
Diagnostic status should be correlated with M-Bus client and port setup requirements before deriving implementation defects.
behavior_notes: Use this class when requirements mention M-Bus error counters, communication diagnostics, received/transmitted frame counts, or resetting diagnostics.
Diagnostics requirements should not be merged with ordinary measurement capture requirements; they describe communication health.

entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 100, version = 0) for setting up time synchronisation using the NTP protocol as specified in RFC 5905. One or several instances may be configured to support multiple time servers. Cardinality 0...n.

access_semantics: All attributes are static, read-write (RW) via SET by an authorised management client; logical_name read-only for all.
activated toggles NTP synchronisation on/off (default FALSE).
server_address may be DNS name, IPv4 dotted-decimal, or IPv6 text.
authentication_keys and client_key are sensitive cryptographic material.
behavior_notes: NTP setup allows time synchronisation via NTP (RFC 5905). Cardinality 0...n; multiple instances support multiple time servers.
activated: boolean, TRUE = sync active, default FALSE.
server_port: default 123 (IANA ntp 123/udp).
synchronize method triggers synchronisation; add/delete_authentication_key manage auth keys.
authentication_method: enum defining NTP authentication mode.

entries.name: ZigBee network control
entries.definition: COSEM interface class (class_id = 104, version = 0). Provides interaction between a DLMS client (head-end system) and a ZigBee® coordinator, e.g. when commissioning the installation. There is a single instance in any device that can act as a ZigBee® coordinator controlled by the DLMS client.
access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.
logical_name (attribute 1) is read-only for all clients.
Per-attribute access rights follow the COSEM access model and the association's access_rights_list; the Blue Book IC table specifies static/dynamic only, not concrete R/RW per client.
behavior_notes: Holds the joining flag and timeout and the list of active (authorised) devices; the methods drive the coordinator to register/unregister devices, manage PAN backup/restore, key updates and PAN create/remove.

Specific methods: register_device (mandatory), unregister_device (mandatory), unregister_all_devices (optional), backup_PAN (optional), restore_PAN (optional), identify_device (optional), remove_mirror (optional), update_network_key (optional), update_link_key (optional), create_PAN (mandatory), remove_PAN (mandatory).
entries.coverage_note: Enriched 2026-06-28 from Blue Book Part 2 Ed.16 section 4.15.5; attributes (id/name/static-or-dynamic/data-type/short-name) and methods taken verbatim from the IC table. access_rights are described semantically, not hard-coded per attribute, because the IC table does not specify concrete R/RW per client.

access_semantics: Static attributes are configuration parameters set via SET by an authorised management client; dynamic attributes are read (GET) status/diagnostic values.

behavior_notes: Holds the tunnel transfer-size limits (incoming/outgoing), the protocol address and the close-tunnel timeout (seconds, restarted on each received command).
Specific methods: none defined.
